title: psorospermum albidum
intro: Psorospermum albidum, also known as the yellow oleander or Cassia alata, is a plant species within the Apocynaceae family, known for its toxic properties. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Psorospermum albidum include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, although it is essential to use it under medical supervision due to its toxicity. Herbal preparations of Psorospermum albidum are typically made by boiling the leaves and roots, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to create decoctions and infusions.
